codex_consult.feedback_summary: codex exec blocked by QA tool-policy (Write denied for role=qa). Self-review substituted per graceful-fallback contract.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: self-review substituted — 5 adversarial questions generated and addressed; no blocking issues found

self_review_adversarial.question: Does OBJ-1 resolution actually prove 9 removed-* files, or just assert the count?
self_review_adversarial.answer: ticket.md line 62 lists 9 individual timestamps inline. context.json scope_expansion.additional_found entry lists the same 9 timestamps. Count is derivable from enumerated items, not just asserted.

self_review_adversarial.question: For OBJ-2, are lines 176-177 confirmed by actual file content, or only BA's claim?
self_review_adversarial.answer: QA independently read hooks/pretool-claude-config-guard.py lines 168-184. Line 176='if _sentinel_present():'; line 177='    sys.exit(0)'. Directly verified by live file read.

self_review_adversarial.question: For OBJ-3, could there be additional sentinel files not in BA's inventory?
self_review_adversarial.answer: BA now documents all 10 instances. Finding 4 table includes orchestra (mtime 2026-05-10), travel-planner (mtime 2026-05-06), .archives, .claude.bak — all previously missing from iter-1. System-wide find scope is stated. Investigation appears complete.

self_review_adversarial.question: For OBJ-4, does BA actually demonstrate HOW condition 1 at :133 grants bypass?
self_review_adversarial.answer: ticket.md Finding 2 reproduces the exact shell condition verbatim from line 133 and explains it evaluates to true for each of 4 live projects. States 'Confirmed by live hook invocation: hook exits 0 for all four.' AC6 provides bash verification command with expected_count=4. OBJ-4 is resolved.

self_review_adversarial.question: For OBJ-5, do the 7 AC contents in ticket.md and acceptance-criteria.json actually match?
self_review_adversarial.answer: Both were read. Both have 7 ACs. Key content points match: AC1=10 total instances (8+2), AC3='enforcement bypass gate at lines 176-177', AC5='9 files' for .removed-*, AC6=condition-1 per-project risk, AC7=post-deletion bypass-closed test. All 7 ACs align.

objections.finding_iter1: .removed-* count was stated as 8 without listing individual files.
objections.resolution_evidence: ticket.md line 62 lists 9 individual timestamps: 1777491091, 17774916892, 1777492074, 1777492671, 1777492689, 1777492723, 1777493861, 1777493887, 1777938221. context-20260521-090300.json scope_expansion.additional_found repeats the same 9 timestamps. Count derivable from enumerated items.

objections.finding_iter1: BA cited lines 47/77 as the enforcement gate. Line 77 is only the _sentinel_present() function body; the actual sys.exit(0) bypass is at lines 176-177 inside main().
objections.resolution_evidence: ticket.md lines 65-66 now state 'actual enforcement gate (if _sentinel_present(): sys.exit(0)) at lines 176-177'. AC3 in acceptance-criteria JSON states 'enforcement bypass gate at lines 176-177'. context.json component_chain[0] has line_range '47,75-77,176-177'. QA live file read confirmed: line 176='if _sentinel_present():'; line 177='    sys.exit(0)'.

objections.finding_iter1: BA's iter-1 inventory did not include /root/orchestra/.claude/.hook-refactor-allow, /root/travel-planner/.claude/.hook-refactor-allow, or archive/backup instances.
objections.resolution_evidence: ticket.md Finding 4 table now lists all 10 instances with paths, mtimes, and risk levels. Explicitly includes orchestra (mtime 2026-05-10 09:08:24, HIGH), travel-planner (mtime 2026-05-06 11:04:39, HIGH), .claude.bak (LOW), .archives (LOW), and 2 Unicode-suffix (INERT). AC1 names all 8 exact-name instances. context.json scope_expansion.all_occurrences lists all 10.

objections.finding_iter1: BA characterized per-project sentinels as lower-risk and did not show they also activate pretool-block-production-files.sh bypass via condition 1 at :133.
objections.resolution_evidence: ticket.md Finding 2 Hook B section quotes the exact shell condition at :133 verbatim and explains condition 1 fires when CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR=/root/happy-dev (and similarly for orchestra, travel-planner, multi-asset-portfolio). States 'Confirmed by live hook invocation: hook exits 0 for all four.' Finding 4 re-assessment: 'all live project sentinels activate pretool-block-production-files.sh bypass in their project context.' context.json R5 AC: '4 live per-project sentinels HIGH-RISK (NOT lower-risk than primary).' AC6 provides bash verification command expecting count=4.

objections.finding_iter1: Ticket.md had 6 ACs; acceptance-criteria.json had 7 ACs. AC7 existed in JSON but not ticket.
objections.resolution_evidence: ticket-20260521-090300.md now has 7 ACs (AC1-AC7). acceptance-criteria-20260521-090300.json has 7 ACs (AC1-AC7). Content verified as matching across all 7.

dimension_1_evidence_quality.notes: All factual claims have evidence. 9 .removed-* timestamps individually enumerated in both documents. 10 filesystem instances each listed with path and mtime. Line citations exact at 47/75-77/176-177. Live-hook-invocation claim backed by context.json Python trace. One minor stale-metadata issue: context.json reference_source.url_or_location says '9 filesystem instances' (should be 10). Single metadata field left stale when count was bumped; body correctly states 10 everywhere. Does not affect any AC.

dimension_2_scope_alignment.notes: User requirement has 6 directives: locate, read contents, identify hooks, determine behavior, assess risk, recommend remediation. BA R1-R6 map 1:1. Won't Have excludes structural redesign, hook code modification, external repos — none exclude anything user asked for.

dimension_3_investigation_completeness.notes: User asked to 'identify every hook file'. BA grep is system-wide across hooks/ confirming exactly 2 hooks. R3 AC requires exhaustive grep command. Investigation found and documented both hooks with complete line citations.

dimension_4_affected_file_accuracy.notes: Both hook files are real (confirmed by QA live read). Line citations 47/75-77/176-177 accurate per live inspection. All 10 filesystem instances have individual paths. No phantom files.

dimension_5_spec_text_vs_execution_drift.notes: AC3 command (grep -rln + grep -n) directly executes what the AC then-clause asserts. AC5 command (ls glob | wc -l) directly measures what the AC count declares. AC6 command (bash for loop) directly tests what the AC then-clause asserts. No substitution of adjacent-but-different verification detected.

qa.overall_assessment: ITER-2 VERDICT: PASS. All 5 prior objections are resolved with concrete evidence. OBJ-1: 9 .removed-* timestamps individually enumerated in both ticket and context JSON. OBJ-2: lines 176-177 enforcement gate confirmed by live file read (line 176='if _sentinel_present():'; line 177='    sys.exit(0)'). OBJ-3: all 10 instances now inventoried including orchestra (mtime 2026-05-10), travel-planner (mtime 2026-05-06), and archive/backup copies — all previously missing. OBJ-4: per-project sentinel risk via condition 1 at :133 explicitly documented with code path citation and live-hook-invocation evidence; all 4 projects shown to carry the bypass. OBJ-5: ticket.md and acceptance-criteria.json both have exactly 7 ACs with matching content. No new blocking issues from 5-dimension check on iter-2 deliverables. One non-blocking out-of-scope observation: context.json reference_source.url_or_location metadata field says '9 filesystem instances' (body correctly says 10 everywhere). BA-QA loop done; orchestrator proceeds to Dev.

out_of_scope_observations.observation: context.json reference_source.url_or_location reads 'stat output for all 9 filesystem instances' but the body correctly documents 10 instances (8 exact-name + 2 Unicode-suffix). Single metadata field left stale when Codex finding bumped count from 9 to 10. Does not affect any AC verification command and has no operational impact.
